growth_records.cite: Meng Wang et al. TCA cycle enhancement and uptake of monomeric substrates support growth of marine Roseobacter at low temperature. Communications Biology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s42003-022-03631-2. [PMC9283371]
growth_records.snippet: At the Topt, the maximum biomass represented by OD600 was 1.772 ± 0.034 for 1A07893 and 1.765 ± 0.043 for 1A07899, and the μmax was 1.333 ± 0.219 d−1 for 1A07893 and 1.879 ± 0.092 d−1 for 1A07899. ... their optimal growth temperature (Topt)
growth_records.notes: Reported μmax = 1.333 ± 0.219 d^-1 at Topt=25 C (marine broth 2216). Unit conversion to per-hour: μ(h^-1)=1.333/24=0.0556 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/μmax=0.69315/1.333=0.520 d = 0.520×24=12.48 h. μmax fitted from OD600 growth curves with Gompertz model (DMFit v3.5) by the authors. Marine broth 2216 is a commercial complex (undefined) medium; composition not itemized in the paper. Max biomass OD600=1.772±0.034 at 25 C. No substrate uptake or product secretion rate reported for the marine-broth condition.

growth_records.cite: Meng Wang et al. TCA cycle enhancement and uptake of monomeric substrates support growth of marine Roseobacter at low temperature. Communications Biology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s42003-022-03631-2. [PMC9283371]
growth_records.snippet: At the Topt, the maximum biomass represented by OD600 was 1.772 ± 0.034 for 1A07893 and 1.765 ± 0.043 for 1A07899, and the μmax was 1.333 ± 0.219 d−1 for 1A07893 and 1.879 ± 0.092 d−1 for 1A07899.
growth_records.notes: Reported μmax = 1.879 ± 0.092 d^-1 at Topt=25 C (marine broth 2216). Unit conversion: μ(h^-1)=1.879/24=0.0783 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/μmax=0.69315/1.879=0.369 d = 0.369×24=8.85 h. μmax fitted from OD600 curves with Gompertz model (DMFit v3.5). Marine broth 2216 is a commercial complex medium; not itemized. Max biomass OD600=1.765±0.043 at 25 C. No uptake/secretion rate reported for the marine-broth condition.

growth_records.medium: Defined artificial-seawater minimal medium with 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose (competitive carbon-source mixture)

up.units: mM consumed (at maximum OD600, first growth stage)
up.method: measured residual glucose in mixed 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose medium (Fig. 6a)

up.units: mM consumed (during stationary phase, second growth stage)

growth_records.cite: Meng Wang et al. TCA cycle enhancement and uptake of monomeric substrates support growth of marine Roseobacter at low temperature. Communications Biology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s42003-022-03631-2. [PMC9283371]
growth_records.snippet: during the first growth stage of 1A07893, the consumption of glucose was 4.606 ± 0.440 mM at 25 °C, which was significantly (p < 0.005) lower than 7.365 ± 0.215 mM at 5 °C ... During the second growth stage of 1A07893, the consumption of gl
growth_records.notes: Competitive glucose/sucrose experiment, strain 1A07893 at 25 C. Values are AMOUNTS of glucose consumed (mM) from a 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose defined medium, NOT per-biomass-per-time rates; cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h because neither biomass in gDW nor an explicit elapsed time is given (only that first stage = at maximum OD600, second stage = during stationary phase). First growth stage: 4.606 ± 0.440 mM glucose consumed; second growth stage: 0.336 ± 0.322 mM. Sucrose consumption for 1A07893 not reported numerically (stated non-significant between temperatures). Glucose consumed < sucrose is preferred (more glucose consumed than sucrose at both temperatures).

growth_records.medium: Defined artificial-seawater minimal medium with 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose (competitive carbon-source mixture)

up.units: mM consumed (at maximum OD600, first growth stage)
up.method: measured residual glucose in mixed 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose medium (Fig. 6a)

up.units: mM consumed (during stationary phase, second growth stage)

growth_records.cite: Meng Wang et al. TCA cycle enhancement and uptake of monomeric substrates support growth of marine Roseobacter at low temperature. Communications Biology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s42003-022-03631-2. [PMC9283371]
growth_records.snippet: the consumption of glucose was 4.606 ± 0.440 mM at 25 °C, which was significantly (p < 0.005) lower than 7.365 ± 0.215 mM at 5 °C ... the consumption of glucose after the same time was 0.905 ± 0.136 mM at 5 °C
growth_records.notes: Competitive glucose/sucrose experiment, strain 1A07893 at 5 C (cold acclimation). AMOUNTS of glucose consumed (mM) from 10 mM glucose + 10 mM sucrose defined medium, NOT rates; no gDW biomass or explicit time given so no mmol/gDW/h conversion possible. First growth stage (at max OD600): 7.365 ± 0.215 mM glucose consumed; second growth stage (stationary): 0.905 ± 0.136 mM. Glucose preference significantly enhanced at 5 C vs 25 C (more glucose consumed at 5 C). Sucrose values not reported numerically. Strain 1A07899 showed a similar pattern but its numeric consumption values were not reported, so no records were made for 1A07899 in the defined medium.
